name: Martinique (France, 2019-2021)

colors.symbolism: the steep hills (mornes) and lush nature of the island
the Atlantic Ocean and the Caribbean Sea

description: A field of eight alternating green and blue segments formed by white horizontal, vertical and diagonal dividing lines; at the centre a white disc bearing a blue lambi conch, encircled by 34 small Amerindian stars and a thin ring of eight arc segments, both alternating green and blue.
history: Named Ipséité ('selfhood') and designed by Johnny Vigne, the flag was selected after two public consultations and unveiled on 10 May 2019 by Alfred Marie-Jeanne, president of the Executive Council, to represent Martinique at international cultural and sporting events; it was first flown at the 2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup. The choice was never enacted by a deliberation of the Assembly of Martinique — the organ that alone holds the collectivity's competence over cultural identity — and on 15 November 2021 the administrative tribunal of Martinique annulled the president's decision as illegal on that ground (judgment nos. 1900632-1900635), ending official use; the design remains in occasional use as a cultural symbol. No official proportions or colour specification were published, and official materials and later renderings vary in ratio. Martinique adopted the rouge-vert-noir as its official flag in 2023.

features.symbolism: the eight segments evoke eight of the languages spoken on the island: French, Creole, English, Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, Chinese and Arabic
the lambi (giant conch), used since Amerindian times as a tool, a fish-hook and an instrument sounded from the mornes to announce the great events of life — births, marriages, deaths, and revolts

features.role: 34 Amerindian stars around the central disc, alternating green and blue
